hero.title: Pub Marketing for Hampshire Pubs
hero.subtitle: Hampshire has around 1,400 pubs serving everything from coastal tourists to New Forest walkers and Southampton students. Seasonality is real — here's how to build trade that lasts all year.
hero.ctaText: Hi Peter, I run a pub in Hampshire and need help
hero.bottomText: £75/hour + VAT • Practical help from a working licensee

intro.heading: What works in Hampshire (and what doesn't)
paragraphs: Hampshire's pub market is shaped by geography. Coastal pubs in Portsmouth and Southsea get summer tourist spikes but can struggle in winter. New Forest pubs rely on holiday visitors and walkers. Test Valley and Winchester pubs serve a mix of locals, commuters, and heritage tourists.

The biggest challenge for most Hampshire pubs? Seasonality. Summer fills your garden and your till, but January to March can be brutal. The pubs that survive year-round are the ones that build a local following strong enough to carry them through the quiet months.

We build systems that work with Hampshire's rhythm: summer campaigns for coastal and New Forest trade, cruise terminal traffic for Southampton pubs, student-friendly offers for Winchester and Portsmouth, and a strong local core that keeps the lights on through winter.

wins.heading: What's working for Hampshire pubs right now

items.title: Summer coastal and New Forest trade
items.description: When tourists search 'pub near Lymington' or 'beer garden Southsea', you need to be the first result. Strong Google photos, seasonal posts, and clear family-friendly messaging make the difference.

items.title: Cruise terminal and port trade
items.description: Southampton's cruise terminal brings thousands of passengers looking for a meal before boarding or after docking. Pre-cruise lunch packages and clear 'near the port' positioning can fill quiet afternoons.

items.title: Build a winter-proof local following
items.description: Tourist trade vanishes in winter. A strong quiz night, a loyal WhatsApp group, and a midweek food offer give you the reliable core trade that carries you from January to Easter.

items.title: Student and young professional trade
items.description: Portsmouth and Winchester have large student populations. Affordable midweek offers, live sport, and social media that speaks their language can fill your quietest nights.

links.title: Start with pub marketing fundamentals
links.description: A practical overview and the best next page based on your biggest problem.

links.title: Build midweek momentum
links.description: Systems to build regulars and consistent midweek trade — essential for surviving Hampshire's off-season.

links.title: Stop falling trade
links.description: A focused 30-day plan to get momentum back when seasonal dips hit hard.

faqs.question: Can you help with seasonal trade swings in Hampshire?
faqs.answer: Yes — that's one of the most common problems we solve. We build a year-round calendar: summer campaigns for tourist and coastal trade, autumn events for locals, winter quiz and food nights, and spring re-launch promotions. The goal is a strong local core that carries you through the quiet months.

faqs.question: Do you work with coastal pubs and New Forest venues?
faqs.answer: Yes. Coastal and New Forest pubs have a specific challenge: converting summer visitors into year-round customers. We use WhatsApp lists, email capture, and review strategies to turn one-time visitors into returning regulars.

faqs.question: Can you help a pub near Southampton's cruise terminal?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Cruise passengers search for 'pub near Southampton port' and 'lunch before cruise'. We optimise your Google profile, create pre-cruise packages, and build partnerships with local taxi firms and hotels to capture that trade.

cta.title: Want more customers through the door in Hampshire?
cta.subtitle: Tell me whether you're coastal, New Forest, or inland — I'll give you the right approach for your specific Hampshire market.
cta.whatsappMessage: Hi Peter, I run a pub in Hampshire and I need help with marketing. Can we have a quick chat?
